headline: MARA's RM1.5 billion London property scandal: zero convictions after 8 years
context: Eight years after the MARA Inc London property acquisition scandal involving RM1.5 billion, no individual has been convicted despite MACC completing its investigation in 2020.

cards.big: RM1.5 billion MARA London property scandal — 8 years, zero convictions
cards.sub: Investigation completed in 2020 but prosecution has stalled indefinitely

cards.big: MACC submitted investigation papers to AGC in December 2020 for prosecution decision
cards.sub: Attorney General's Chambers has held the file for over 5 years without action

cards.big: MARA overpaid an estimated RM400 million above market value for London properties
cards.sub: Bumiputera development funds used to enrich intermediaries through inflated prices

cards.big: 3 MARA directors identified in investigation still hold government advisory positions
cards.sub: Suspects remain in positions of public trust while prosecution decisions are deferred

cards.big: Justice delayed by 8 years isn't pending — it's a quiet decision not to prosecute
Should AGC be required to make prosecution decisions within 12 months of receiving investigation papers?
